sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 0.4g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a vegetable, low downside and high payoff is what you want.
sections.subtitleText: 0.4g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a vegetable, low downside and high payoff is what you want.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 3.6g of carbs. starch is doing most of the heavy lifting here. for vegetables, low downside is useful, but there still needs to be real payoff.
sections.subtitleText: 3.6g of carbs. starch is doing most of the heavy lifting here. for vegetables, low downside is useful, but there still needs to be real payoff.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 2.9g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is not the main pitch here, but extra support still matters.
sections.subtitleText: 2.9g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is not the main pitch here, but extra support still matters.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin k at 402% daily value, vitamin a at 56% daily value, vitamin c at 31% daily value stand out most. that is major vitamin support.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin k at 402% daily value, vitamin a at 56% daily value, vitamin c at 31% daily value stand out most. that is major vitamin support.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: potassium at 12% daily value, magnesium at 19% daily value, iron at 15% daily value stand out most. useful, but still pretty limited.
sections.subtitleText: potassium at 12% daily value, magnesium at 19% daily value, iron at 15% daily value stand out most. useful, but still pretty limited.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side: elite micronutrient density. this is exactly the kind of nutrient-packed vegetable the category is supposed to reward. very light for the volume. a practical plus for a maintenance-and-repair food. easy to use in lots of meals. works raw, cooked, blended, and as a side or add-in.

displayItems.explanation: This is exactly the kind of nutrient-packed vegetable the category is supposed to reward.

displayItems.title: very light for the volume
displayItems.explanation: A practical plus for a maintenance-and-repair food.

displayItems.title: easy to use in lots of meals
displayItems.explanation: Works raw, cooked, blended, and as a side or add-in.

sections.subtitleText: on the plus side: elite micronutrient density. this is exactly the kind of nutrient-packed vegetable the category is supposed to reward. very light for the volume. a practical plus for a maintenance-and-repair food. easy to use in lots of meals. works raw, cooked, blended, and as a side or add-in.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides: contains anti-nutrients. oxalates can reduce mineral usefulness in some contexts. volume shrinks a lot when cooked. can make portions feel smaller than expected. not useful as a fuel food. that is fine for vegetables, but it does limit some use cases.

closing.summary: Spinach has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want low downside and easy micronutrient support.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want low downside and easy micronutrient support.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Carbs are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in S tier.
